title: SEC3: internal notification path double-serializes JSON
description: transport.rs / client.rs: The internal notification pipeline passes data as raw JSON strings across the Rust-to-Rust boundary between transport and client. transport.rs receives the text frame, parses it to `Value`, decides it is a notification, then passes the ORIGINAL text string (not the parsed Value) to the notify callback (line 116: `JsValue::from_str(&text)`). client.rs then re-parses that string from_str a second time (line 127). This is a parse-serialize-reparse round-trip within one Rust process. The transport's internal notify callback type should be `Rc<dyn Fn(Value)>` so the already-parsed `Value` is passed directly. Serialization back to a string should happen only once, at the JS boundary in api.rs when calling the user's JS Function.
close_reason: duplicate of nie-7bv6.1: both report the double JSON parse in the transport→client notification path; nie-7bv6.1 is the primary P2 efficiency finding
